CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str_val VARCHAR(255));
SELECT lpad(str_val, 6) AS padded_str FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT lpad(str_val, 10) AS padded_str FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT lpad(str_val, 5) AS padded_str FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT lpad(str_val, 15) AS padded_str FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT lpad(str_val, 6) FROM (SELECT str_val FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1) AS subquery;
SELECT id, lpad(str_val, 6) AS padded_str FROM test_strings ORDER BY id;
SELECT lpad(str_val, 6) FROM (SELECT str_val FROM test_strings WHERE LENGTH(str_val) < 10) AS filtered;
SELECT id, lpad(CONCAT(str_val, '!'), 10) AS padded_str FROM test_strings;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE lpad(str_val, 10) = '     abc';
CREATE TABLE extra_strings (id INT, str_val_2 VARCHAR(255));
SELECT a.id, lpad(a.str_val, 6) AS padded_str_a, lpad(b.str_val_2, 10) AS padded_str_b FROM test_strings a JOIN extra_strings b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT DISTINCT lpad(str_val, 8) FROM test_strings WHERE str_val LIKE 'h%';
SELECT lpad(str_val, 10) AS padded_str FROM (SELECT str_val FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM extra_strings)) AS subquery;
